commands.name: git init
commands.description: Create an empty Git repository or reinitialize an existing one

patterns.text: start a new git repository in the current directory
patterns.explanation: Initializes a new .git directory in the current working directory

patterns.text: create a new git repo in a specific folder
patterns.explanation: Creates the directory my-project and initializes a git repo inside it

patterns.text: initialize a bare repository for a server
patterns.explanation: Creates a bare repository without a working directory, typically used on servers

patterns.text: start a repo with main as the default branch
patterns.explanation: Initializes a repo and sets the initial branch name to main instead of master

patterns.text: reinitialize an existing repository to pick up new templates
patterns.explanation: Running git init in an existing repo is safe and can add new templates

commands.name: git clone
commands.description: Clone a repository into a new directory

patterns.text: download a repository from GitHub
patterns.explanation: Clones the remote repository into a local directory named repo

patterns.text: clone a repo into a specific folder name
patterns.explanation: Clones the repo but names the local directory my-folder

patterns.text: shallow clone only the latest commit to save time
patterns.explanation: Creates a shallow clone with only the most recent commit history

patterns.text: clone a specific branch from a repository
patterns.explanation: Clones the repo and checks out the develop branch

patterns.text: clone a repo using SSH
patterns.explanation: Clones using SSH protocol for authentication

patterns.text: clone a repo including all submodules
patterns.explanation: Clones the repo and initializes all submodules recursively

patterns.text: clone only a single branch to save bandwidth
patterns.explanation: Clones only the main branch without fetching other branches

commands.name: git add
commands.description: Add file contents to the staging area (index)

patterns.text: stage a specific file for commit
patterns.explanation: Adds filename.txt to the staging area

patterns.text: stage all changed and new files
patterns.explanation: Stages all changes including new, modified, and deleted files

patterns.text: stage all changes in the current directory
patterns.explanation: Adds all changes in the current directory and subdirectories

patterns.text: interactively choose which hunks to stage
patterns.explanation: Lets you review and selectively stage parts of files

patterns.text: stage only modified and deleted files, not new ones
patterns.explanation: Stages modifications and deletions but ignores untracked files

patterns.text: stage all Python files
patterns.explanation: Uses a glob pattern to stage all Python files

commands.name: git commit
commands.description: Record changes to the repository

patterns.text: save staged changes with a message
patterns.explanation: Creates a commit with the given message

patterns.text: stage all tracked changes and commit in one step
patterns.explanation: Automatically stages modified tracked files and commits

patterns.text: change the last commit message
patterns.explanation: Replaces the most recent commit with a new message

patterns.text: add forgotten files to the last commit
patterns.explanation: Adds currently staged files to the previous commit without changing its message

patterns.text: create an empty commit for triggering CI
patterns.explanation: Creates a commit with no changes, useful for triggering pipelines

patterns.text: commit with a specific author
patterns.explanation: Overrides the default author for this commit

patterns.text: commit and sign with GPG key
patterns.explanation: Creates a GPG-signed commit for verification

commands.name: git push
commands.description: Update remote refs along with associated objects

patterns.text: upload local commits to the remote repository
patterns.explanation: Pushes the current branch to the configured upstream remote

patterns.text: push a branch to origin for the first time
patterns.explanation: Pushes the branch and sets up tracking for future pushes

patterns.text: force push to overwrite remote history
patterns.explanation: Overwrites the remote branch with local history, use with caution

patterns.text: safely force push only if no one else has pushed
patterns.explanation: Force pushes but fails if the remote has commits you haven't seen

patterns.text: push all local branches to the remote
patterns.explanation: Pushes every local branch to the origin remote

patterns.text: push tags to the remote repository
patterns.explanation: Pushes all local tags to the remote

patterns.text: delete a remote branch
patterns.explanation: Removes the specified branch from the remote repository

commands.name: git pull
commands.description: Fetch from and integrate with another repository or local branch

patterns.text: download and merge remote changes
patterns.explanation: Fetches and merges changes from the upstream branch

patterns.text: pull changes and rebase instead of merge
patterns.explanation: Fetches changes and replays your commits on top

patterns.text: pull from a specific remote branch
patterns.explanation: Fetches and merges changes from origin/main

patterns.text: pull and automatically stash local changes
patterns.explanation: Stashes local changes before pulling and reapplies them after

patterns.text: pull only if it can fast-forward
patterns.explanation: Only updates if the merge can be fast-forwarded, fails otherwise

commands.name: git fetch
commands.description: Download objects and refs from another repository

patterns.text: download remote changes without merging
patterns.explanation: Fetches all branches from the default remote without modifying working directory

patterns.text: fetch from all remotes
patterns.explanation: Downloads updates from every configured remote

patterns.text: fetch and prune deleted remote branches
patterns.explanation: Removes local references to branches that no longer exist on the remote

patterns.text: fetch a specific branch from origin
patterns.explanation: Downloads only the main branch from origin

patterns.text: fetch tags from the remote
patterns.explanation: Downloads all tags from the remote repository

commands.name: git merge
commands.description: Join two or more development histories together

patterns.text: merge another branch into the current one
patterns.explanation: Integrates changes from feature-branch into the current branch

patterns.text: merge without creating a merge commit
patterns.explanation: Only merges if a fast-forward is possible, keeping linear history

patterns.text: always create a merge commit even for fast-forwards
patterns.explanation: Creates a merge commit even when fast-forward is possible

patterns.text: merge but squash all commits into one
patterns.explanation: Combines all feature branch commits into a single staged change

patterns.text: abort a merge that has conflicts
patterns.explanation: Cancels the merge and restores the pre-merge state

patterns.text: continue a merge after resolving conflicts
patterns.explanation: Proceeds with the merge after you have resolved all conflicts

commands.name: git rebase
commands.description: Reapply commits on top of another base tip

patterns.text: rebase current branch onto main
patterns.explanation: Replays current branch commits on top of main

patterns.text: interactively reorder, squash, or edit commits
patterns.explanation: Opens an editor to manipulate the last 5 commits

patterns.text: abort a rebase that has conflicts
patterns.explanation: Cancels the rebase and returns to the original state

patterns.text: continue rebase after resolving a conflict
patterns.explanation: Proceeds with the rebase after conflict resolution

patterns.text: skip a conflicting commit during rebase
patterns.explanation: Skips the current commit and continues rebasing

patterns.text: rebase onto a specific commit
patterns.explanation: Replays commits from start-commit to end-branch onto target-branch

commands.name: git branch
commands.description: List, create, or delete branches

patterns.text: list all local branches
patterns.explanation: Shows all local branches with the current one highlighted

patterns.text: list all branches including remote ones
patterns.explanation: Shows both local and remote tracking branches

patterns.text: create a new branch
patterns.explanation: Creates a new branch at the current commit without switching to it

patterns.text: delete a branch that has been merged
patterns.explanation: Deletes the branch only if it has been fully merged

patterns.text: force delete a branch even if not merged
patterns.explanation: Forcefully deletes the branch regardless of merge status

patterns.text: rename the current branch
patterns.explanation: Renames the current branch to new-name

patterns.text: see which branches contain a specific commit
patterns.explanation: Lists branches that include the specified commit

commands.name: git checkout
commands.description: Switch branches or restore working tree files

patterns.text: switch to an existing branch
patterns.explanation: Switches the working directory to the main branch

patterns.text: create and switch to a new branch
patterns.explanation: Creates a new branch and switches to it immediately

patterns.text: discard changes to a specific file
patterns.explanation: Restores the file to the last committed version

patterns.text: check out a file from another branch
patterns.explanation: Copies the file from another branch into the current working directory

patterns.text: go to a specific commit in detached HEAD state
patterns.explanation: Moves HEAD to the specified commit without being on a branch

patterns.text: create a branch from a remote branch and track it
patterns.explanation: Creates a local branch based on a remote branch

commands.name: git switch
commands.description: Switch branches (modern replacement for git checkout for branch switching)

patterns.text: switch to another branch
patterns.explanation: Switches to the main branch

patterns.text: create and switch to a new branch
patterns.explanation: Creates and switches to a new branch

patterns.text: switch back to the previous branch
patterns.explanation: Toggles back to the previously checked-out branch

patterns.text: switch to a remote branch and track it
patterns.explanation: Creates a local branch that tracks the remote branch and switches to it

patterns.text: switch to a detached HEAD at a tag
patterns.explanation: Moves to the tagged commit in detached HEAD state

commands.name: git log
commands.description: Show commit logs

patterns.text: show the commit history
patterns.explanation: Displays the full commit log for the current branch

patterns.text: show a compact one-line-per-commit history
patterns.explanation: Displays abbreviated commit hashes and messages one per line

patterns.text: show a visual graph of branches and merges
patterns.explanation: Draws an ASCII graph showing branch topology

patterns.text: show commits by a specific author
patterns.explanation: Filters the log to only show commits by John

patterns.text: show commits from the last week
patterns.explanation: Displays only commits made within the last week

patterns.text: show the history of a specific file
patterns.explanation: Shows all commits that modified the file, even across renames

patterns.text: search commit messages for a keyword
patterns.explanation: Shows only commits whose messages contain bugfix

patterns.text: show the next commit after a specific commit hash
patterns.explanation: Prints the hash of the commit that directly follows <hash> on the current branch

patterns.text: show full details of the commit right after a given hash
patterns.explanation: Displays the diff and message of the commit immediately after <hash>

patterns.text: list all commits after a hash in chronological order
patterns.explanation: Shows commits on main that come after <hash>, oldest first

patterns.text: visualize commits that come after a hash
patterns.explanation: Draws an ASCII graph of commits on the path from <hash> to HEAD

patterns.text: show commits in a range between two hashes
patterns.explanation: Lists commits on the path between two specific hashes, excluding <old-hash>

commands.name: git rev-list
commands.description: List commit objects in reverse chronological order, useful for scripting and traversing history

patterns.text: find the direct children of a commit
patterns.explanation: Prints <hash> followed by the hashes of all commits that have it as a parent

patterns.text: count the number of commits on the current branch
patterns.explanation: Returns the total number of commits reachable from HEAD

patterns.text: count commits between two references
patterns.explanation: Shows how many commits the current branch is ahead of main

patterns.text: list all commit hashes in a repository
patterns.explanation: Prints every commit hash reachable from any ref in the repo

patterns.text: find the first commit in the repository
patterns.explanation: Returns the root commit(s) — commits with no parents

patterns.text: list commits that touched a specific file
patterns.explanation: Shows hashes of every commit that modified the given file

commands.name: git diff
commands.description: Show changes between commits, working tree, and staging area

patterns.text: see what has changed but is not yet staged
patterns.explanation: Shows unstaged modifications in the working directory

patterns.text: see what is staged and ready to commit
patterns.explanation: Shows changes that have been added to the staging area

patterns.text: compare two branches
patterns.explanation: Shows the differences between the two branches

patterns.text: see a summary of changed files only
patterns.explanation: Shows a compact summary of files changed and lines added/removed

patterns.text: compare a specific file between two commits
patterns.explanation: Shows changes to a specific file between two commits

patterns.text: show word-level differences instead of line-level
patterns.explanation: Highlights individual changed words rather than whole lines

patterns.text: get a one-line summary of total changes
patterns.explanation: Prints a single line like: X files changed, Y insertions(+), Z deletions(-)

patterns.text: get machine-readable numeric stats for each changed file
patterns.explanation: Outputs tab-separated added<TAB>deleted<TAB>filename, ideal for scripting

patterns.text: see percentage of changes per directory
patterns.explanation: Shows the percentage of changes grouped by directory

patterns.text: see dirstat with cumulative percentages and file counts
patterns.explanation: Groups by directory using file counts, hides dirs under 10%, and includes parent dirs cumulatively

patterns.text: list changed files with their status letter
patterns.explanation: Outputs each file prefixed with A (added), M (modified), D (deleted), or R (renamed)

patterns.text: count changed files per top-level folder
patterns.command: git diff --name-only main..HEAD | awk -F/ '{print $1}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn
patterns.explanation: Simple per-folder file count without line stats, sorted by most-changed folder first

patterns.text: aggregate files changed and lines added/removed per top-level folder
patterns.command: git diff --numstat main..HEAD | awk '{split($3,a,"/"); files[a[1]]++; add[a[1]]+=$1; del[a[1]]+=$2} END {for (d in files) printf "%-30s %5d files  +%-6d -%d\n", d, files[d], add[d], del[d]}' | sort -k2 -nr
patterns.explanation: Rolls up numstat into per-top-level-folder totals: files changed, insertions, deletions

patterns.text: aggregate stats per folder at any depth (configurable)
patterns.command: git diff --numstat main..HEAD | awk -v depth=3 '{n=split($3,a,"/"); lim=(n<depth)?n:depth; d=a[1]; for (i=2; i<=lim; i++) d=d"/"a[i]; files[d]++; add[d]+=$1; del[d]+=$2} END {for (d in files) printf "%-50s %5d  +%-6d -%d\n", d, files[d], add[d], del[d]}' | sort -k2 -nr
patterns.explanation: Per-folder stats grouped at any path depth — change -v depth=3 to the desired number of levels

patterns.text: show lines added and removed per author across a range
patterns.command: git log --numstat --pretty=format:"%an" main..HEAD | awk 'NF==1{author=$0} NF==3{add[author]+=$1; del[author]+=$2} END {for (a in add) printf "%-25s +%-8d -%d\n", a, add[a], del[a]}'
patterns.explanation: Aggregates insertions and deletions by commit author across the given range

commands.name: git status
commands.description: Show the working tree status

patterns.text: check which files have been modified or staged
patterns.explanation: Displays the state of the working directory and staging area

patterns.text: show a short compact status
patterns.explanation: Displays status in a short format with status codes

patterns.text: show status including the branch tracking info
patterns.explanation: Short format status with branch and tracking information

patterns.text: check status but ignore untracked files
patterns.explanation: Shows status but omits untracked files from the output

patterns.text: show status with paths relative to the repo root
patterns.explanation: Machine-readable output format, useful for scripting

commands.name: git stash
commands.description: Stash the changes in a dirty working directory away

patterns.text: temporarily save my uncommitted changes
patterns.explanation: Saves all modified tracked files and staged changes to a stack

patterns.text: stash changes with a descriptive message
patterns.command: git stash push -m "work in progress on login"
patterns.explanation: Saves changes with a label for easy identification

patterns.text: restore the most recent stashed changes
patterns.explanation: Applies the latest stash and removes it from the stash list

patterns.text: list all stashed changes
patterns.explanation: Shows all entries in the stash stack

patterns.text: apply a stash without removing it from the list
patterns.explanation: Applies the specified stash but keeps it in the stack

patterns.text: stash including untracked files
patterns.explanation: Stashes both tracked and untracked files

patterns.text: drop a specific stash entry
patterns.explanation: Removes a specific stash entry from the stack

commands.name: git reset
commands.description: Reset current HEAD to the specified state

patterns.text: unstage a file but keep the changes
patterns.explanation: Removes the file from the staging area but preserves modifications

patterns.text: undo the last commit but keep changes staged
patterns.explanation: Moves HEAD back one commit, changes remain in staging area

patterns.text: undo the last commit and unstage the changes
patterns.explanation: Moves HEAD back one commit, changes go to the working directory

patterns.text: completely discard the last commit and all changes
patterns.explanation: Permanently removes the last commit and all associated changes

patterns.text: reset to match the remote branch exactly
patterns.explanation: Discards all local commits and changes, matching the remote

patterns.text: unstage all files
patterns.explanation: Removes all files from the staging area without changing the working directory

commands.name: git revert
commands.description: Create a new commit that undoes a previous commit

patterns.text: undo a commit without rewriting history
patterns.explanation: Creates a new commit that reverses the changes from the specified commit

patterns.text: revert the most recent commit
patterns.explanation: Undoes the latest commit by creating a new reverting commit

patterns.text: revert a merge commit
patterns.explanation: Reverts a merge commit, keeping the first parent's side

patterns.text: revert without committing immediately
patterns.explanation: Stages the revert changes but does not create a commit

patterns.text: revert a range of commits
patterns.explanation: Reverts the last three commits one by one

commands.name: git cherry-pick
commands.description: Apply the changes introduced by some existing commits

patterns.text: apply a specific commit from another branch
patterns.explanation: Copies the specified commit to the current branch

patterns.text: cherry-pick without committing
patterns.explanation: Applies the changes but does not create a commit

patterns.text: cherry-pick a range of commits
patterns.explanation: Applies a range of commits to the current branch

patterns.text: abort a cherry-pick that has conflicts
patterns.explanation: Cancels the cherry-pick and restores the original state

patterns.text: continue cherry-pick after resolving conflicts
patterns.explanation: Proceeds with the cherry-pick after resolving conflicts

commands.name: git tag
commands.description: Create, list, delete, or verify tags

patterns.text: list all tags
patterns.explanation: Shows all tags in the repository

patterns.text: create a lightweight tag
patterns.explanation: Creates a simple tag pointing to the current commit

patterns.text: create an annotated tag with a message
patterns.explanation: Creates a tag with metadata including tagger and message

patterns.text: tag a specific older commit
patterns.explanation: Creates an annotated tag on a specific past commit

patterns.text: delete a local tag
patterns.explanation: Removes the tag from the local repository

patterns.text: list tags matching a pattern
patterns.explanation: Shows only tags that match the wildcard pattern

commands.name: git remote
commands.description: Manage set of tracked repositories

patterns.text: list all configured remotes
patterns.explanation: Shows all remotes with their fetch and push URLs

patterns.text: add a new remote repository
patterns.explanation: Registers a new remote named upstream

patterns.text: remove a remote
patterns.explanation: Deletes the specified remote and its tracking branches

patterns.text: rename a remote
patterns.explanation: Changes the name of a remote from origin to upstream

patterns.text: change the URL of an existing remote
patterns.explanation: Updates the URL for the specified remote

patterns.text: show detailed info about a remote
patterns.explanation: Displays branches, tracking info, and push/pull configuration

commands.name: git bisect
commands.description: Use binary search to find the commit that introduced a bug

patterns.text: start binary search for a bug
patterns.explanation: Begins the bisect process to find a bad commit

patterns.text: mark the current commit as having the bug
patterns.explanation: Tells bisect the current commit exhibits the bug

patterns.text: mark a known good commit where the bug did not exist
patterns.explanation: Tells bisect this commit is known to be bug-free

patterns.text: end the bisect session and go back to the original branch
patterns.explanation: Finishes bisecting and returns HEAD to the original position

patterns.text: automate bisect with a test script
patterns.explanation: Runs the script on each step; exit 0 means good, non-zero means bad

commands.name: git blame
commands.description: Show what revision and author last modified each line of a file

patterns.text: see who last changed each line of a file
patterns.explanation: Annotates each line with the commit hash, author, and date

patterns.text: blame a specific range of lines
patterns.explanation: Shows blame information only for lines 10 through 20

patterns.text: ignore whitespace changes in blame
patterns.explanation: Ignores whitespace when determining the last change

patterns.text: detect lines that were moved or copied from other files
patterns.explanation: Traces lines to their original source across file copies

patterns.text: show blame with email addresses instead of names
patterns.explanation: Displays author email addresses in the blame output

commands.name: git show
commands.description: Show various types of objects (commits, tags, trees, blobs)

patterns.text: see the details and diff of a specific commit
patterns.explanation: Displays commit info, author, date, message, and the diff

patterns.text: show only the commit message without the diff
patterns.explanation: Shows commit details with a file change summary

patterns.text: view a file at a specific commit
patterns.explanation: Displays the contents of the file as it existed at that commit

patterns.text: show the details of a tag
patterns.explanation: Displays the tag message and the commit it points to

patterns.text: show the last commit on the current branch
patterns.explanation: Displays the most recent commit details and diff

commands.name: git clean
commands.description: Remove untracked files from the working tree

patterns.text: preview which untracked files would be deleted
patterns.explanation: Dry run showing which files would be removed

patterns.text: delete all untracked files
patterns.explanation: Forcefully removes all untracked files

patterns.text: remove untracked files and directories
patterns.explanation: Removes untracked files and empty untracked directories

patterns.text: remove untracked files including those in .gitignore
patterns.explanation: Removes all untracked files including ignored ones like build artifacts

patterns.text: interactively select which untracked files to remove
patterns.explanation: Walks through each untracked file and asks whether to delete it

commands.name: git worktree
commands.description: Manage multiple working trees attached to the same repository

patterns.text: create a new working directory for a branch
patterns.explanation: Creates a separate working directory checked out to hotfix-branch

patterns.text: list all active worktrees
patterns.explanation: Shows all working trees and which branches they have checked out

patterns.text: remove a worktree that is no longer needed
patterns.explanation: Deletes the worktree directory and its administrative files

patterns.text: create a worktree with a new branch
patterns.explanation: Creates a new branch and a worktree for it simultaneously

patterns.text: clean up stale worktree references
patterns.explanation: Removes worktree entries for directories that no longer exist

commands.name: git submodule
commands.description: Initialize, update, or inspect submodules

patterns.text: add a submodule to the project
patterns.explanation: Adds a repository as a submodule in the libs/lib directory

patterns.text: initialize submodules after cloning
patterns.explanation: Registers submodule paths from .gitmodules in the local config

patterns.text: download and update all submodules
patterns.explanation: Initializes and clones all submodules including nested ones

patterns.text: pull latest changes for all submodules
patterns.explanation: Updates each submodule to the latest commit on its tracked branch

patterns.text: show the status of all submodules
patterns.explanation: Shows the current commit and state of each submodule

patterns.text: run a command in every submodule
patterns.command: git submodule foreach 'git pull origin main'
patterns.explanation: Executes the given command inside each submodule directory

commands.name: git reflog
commands.description: Record and view reference update history

patterns.text: see the history of where HEAD has been
patterns.explanation: Shows a log of all HEAD movements including resets and rebases

patterns.text: recover a commit that was lost after a reset
patterns.explanation: Lists all recent positions of HEAD to find lost commits

patterns.text: show reflog for a specific branch
patterns.explanation: Displays the movement history of the main branch ref

patterns.text: restore to a reflog entry
patterns.explanation: Resets HEAD to where it was 3 moves ago according to the reflog

patterns.text: expire old reflog entries
patterns.explanation: Removes reflog entries older than 90 days

commands.name: git merge-base
commands.description: Find the best common ancestor between commits or branches, useful for identifying where a branch was created

patterns.text: find the commit where a branch was created from main
patterns.explanation: Prints the SHA of the common ancestor commit where feature-branch diverged from main

patterns.text: show the commit that a branch was branched off from
patterns.explanation: Finds the branch point between the current branch and main

patterns.text: find the fork point of a branch after rebases
patterns.explanation: Uses the reflog to find the original branch point, even if the base branch has moved

patterns.text: show the commit details of the branch creation point
patterns.explanation: Displays the full commit info for the point where the branch was created

patterns.text: list all commits on a branch since it was created
patterns.explanation: Shows every commit added on feature-branch after it diverged from main

patterns.text: check if one commit is an ancestor of another
patterns.explanation: Exits 0 if commit1 is an ancestor of commit2, 1 otherwise — useful in scripts

patterns.text: find the common ancestor of multiple branches
patterns.explanation: Returns the best common ancestor across three or more branches for octopus merges

patterns.text: list all merge bases when history has multiple ancestors
patterns.explanation: Shows every possible common ancestor instead of just the best one

patterns.text: find where the current branch diverged from its upstream
patterns.explanation: Identifies the point where the current branch split from its tracked remote branch

patterns.text: get the first commit made on the current branch
patterns.command: git log --oneline $(git merge-base main HEAD)..HEAD | tail -1
patterns.explanation: Shows the earliest commit added on the current branch after it was created from main
